text: Managing a team effectively requires a mix of key skills. First, open communication is essential — you need to listen to your team members and share information clearly. Delegation is also important; assign tasks based on each person's strengths, but avoid overloading anyone. Regular feedback helps people improve and feel valued, so offer both praise and constructive criticism. When conflicts arise, address them quickly by listening to all sides and finding a fair solution. Finally, keep motivation high by celebrating achievements and showing appreciation for hard work. With these practices, a team can stay productive and work well together.
